entities.name: WILD PEA
entities.description: Pisum sativum subsp. elatius, a wild relative of the pea crop used as a source of genetic diversity

entities.name: FERTILE CRESCENT
entities.description: A region in the northern part where wild pea populations were collected

entities.name: CROP GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing crop traits using genetic diversity from wild relatives

entities.name: CROP WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Wild species related to cultivated crops that serve as genepools

entities.name: YOUNGER DRYAS
entities.description: A cold-dry period approximately 12,000 years ago

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Environmental shifts that make certain traits in wild pea useful

entities.name: GENETIC STRUCTURE
entities.description: The organization of genetic variation within and between populations of wild pea

entities.name: DARTSEQ MARKERS
entities.description: 18,397 genome-wide single nucleotide polymorphism markers used to assess genetic structure

entities.name: AMOVA
entities.description: Analysis of Molecular Variance used to determine the distribution of allelic variation

entities.name: ALLELIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity of alleles distributed between populations and individuals

entities.name: HETEROZYGOSITY
entities.description: A measure of genetic variation within individuals, observed between 0.99 to 6.26%

entities.name: SELF-POLLINATION RATE
entities.description: The estimated rate of self-fertilization, ranging from 47 to 90%

entities.name: GEOGRAPHIC DISTANCE
entities.description: The physical distance between populations, which correlates with genetic distance

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL DISTANCE
entities.description: The climatic distance between populations, which does not correlate with genetic distance

entities.name: MIXED MATING SYSTEM
entities.description: A reproductive system involving both self-pollination and cross-pollination

entities.name: NICHE MODELLING
entities.description: A method used to predict the suitability of habitats based on climatic projections

entities.name: FUTURE CLIMATIC PROJECTIONS
entities.description: Predicted climate scenarios used in niche modelling

entities.name: EX SITU CONSERVATION
entities.description: The preservation of genetic resources outside their natural habitats
